what_is_interesting: Marcus Aurelius wrote these meditations as private notes to quiet his own anxious emperor's mind — and 2,000 years later, those same words are still powerful enough to put a modern listener to sleep.
likely_image: A marble bust or portrait of Marcus Aurelius against a deep, dark background — candlelight, stars, or a Roman night sky — evoking ancient calm and solitude.
winner_reasoning: 'STILL CALMS' works on two levels simultaneously — it means 'continues to calm minds today' (timeless power) AND evokes the word 'still' as in quietness and stillness, so paired with a Marcus Aurelius bust in candlelight, the viewer instantly feels: this 2,000-year-old emperor's private thoughts are still doing something to people right now, and I want that.
